title: Babel, The Wrong Woods
stakes: A parallel Earth that fences its own corruption and sells tickets to it, a coma an ocean away that is not death, and a Tower at the south pole that has been left unattended too long.

world.name: Babel
world.flavor: a parallel Earth where a hundred-floor Tower at the south pole leaks Chaos into the green and the running, and the licensed make uneasy money off the corruption they cannot cure
world.era: modern parallel Earth, present-day pre-corruption Pacific Northwest, logging towns, ranger stations, highways, hospitals, hydro dams

start_location.name: The Green Static, Fringe
start_location.description: The near edge of an old Pacific-Northwest rainforest, Exclusion Zone EZ-44. The ground right here is quiet and kept: soft dawn light, moss underfoot, and a clear path running plainly north through the scrub toward the tree-line, where a diesel hum and a flagpole mark a town. Above the far canopy the air shimmers like bad signal, that shimmer is the boundary, and everything wrong waits on the other side of it. Here, on Her kept ground, the paths are honest and the way is easy to read.

second_location.name: Hollow Pine
second_location.description: An old timber town of maybe fourteen hundred souls, an hour past the end of reliable cell coverage, now a licensed frontier outpost making uneasy money off the zone it lives beside. Chain-link and prefab crowd a Victorian main street; a flagpole flies three flags, national, state, and the hunter-association pennant. Grace's licensing office, the Stump bar, Vail's salvage lot, and Doc Han's clinic ring the square.

third_location.name: The Green Static, The Heart
third_location.description: Deeper into EZ-44, where the corruption stops reading as weather and starts reading as intent. The canopy closes; the wrongness thickens to a pressure behind the eyes. This is the Stag's country, the zone's corrupted alpha, and the one soul who lives out here on purpose keeps a cabin somewhere in the dark under the leaves.

loc_waking_mile.name: The Waking Mile
loc_waking_mile.description: The clearest ground in the Verdance, deliberately kept calm by HER. Soft light, honest paths, the corruption pushed politely back. The shimmer at its edge is a boundary marker, and the first step past it is a threshold you feel in your teeth as the STATUS window flickers. Older flattened bedding-spots in the grass show where other champions woke before you.

loc_elkwater_crossing.name: Elkwater Crossing
loc_elkwater_crossing.description: A refugee and trader camp at a river ford, the region's living hub. Watch rotations stand day and night, and funeral flags for the vanished snap over the tents in the wind off the water. Tarps, a plank market, a bunkhouse, and a board thick with notices. The one place in the Verdance where strangers are fed before they are questioned.

services.label: Take a bunk at the crossing

notices.text: MEDICINE, the Aldous children are burning up with the ford-fever and we have nothing left. Anyone brave enough to run to St. Brigid's and back gets everything we can spare. Please., Merrin

IT TOOK THE HALE HOUSE WHOLE. Plates still set, and something walked OUT. The trail's fresh from the Waking Mile and my sight says it's close. Coin and a bunk for whoever ends it before it circles back.
LOST, a surveyor's kit on the Drowned Highway, brass and glass, worth a season's papers. Bring it to the ford, no questions asked.
IF YOU'VE BEEN TO THE TITHING MILL and came back wrong, or came back with more than you carried in, speak to Ruth. Quietly. She's keeping count.
CHARTER ASSESSORS at the ford by week's end. Salvage papers in order or the zone's work goes to the licensed. You have been told.

loc_root_shrine.name: The Root Shrine
loc_root_shrine.description: A keeper-camp at the base of the World-Tree, where monks and wardens read the omens in bark and bloom and quietly enforce the one law that keeps the region alive: take gently. Rope-and-cedar shelters, a fire that is never let out, and elders who will trade lore and rumor for the courtesy of listening. A place to rest without being asked why you came.

services.label: Ask the elders about the region

loc_penitents_row.name: The Penitents' Row
loc_penitents_row.description: The path of ruined poacher camps that climbs toward the Tree, each wreck a lesson in the Ledger: a still crushed under a healthy fallen oak, a trapline swallowed by a season of spiders, a truck that rolled for no reason on flat ground. Nothing here was killed by a demon. Everything here was taken too greedily, too close to Her, and paid for it. The keepers leave it exactly as it lies.

loc_world_tree.name: The World-Tree
loc_world_tree.description: Her anchor, and the region's living health-bar. It rises past where the canopy has any business ending, and its condition, bloom, rot, the slow green glow along the deepest bark, is the land's corruption read as omen, not number, cryptic even to the keepers who spend their lives beneath it. Approach humbly and it is the safest ground in the Verdance. Raise a hand against it and there is not a hero alive who would like your odds.

loc_st_brigids.name: St. Brigid's Regional Medical
loc_st_brigids.description: A rapture-site gone old and quiet: pristine wards, IVs still dripping into empty beds, charts mid-sentence. The demon that emptied it is long gone, its essence trails lead OUT the ambulance doors, the region's first clean track to follow. But the region's only real stock of medicine is still on these shelves, and the looters' cairns stacked at the entrance say plenty of people weighed the risk and went in anyway.

searchDetails.label: the medicine shelves
searchDetails.description: Behind a toppled crash-cart, a locked cabinet the looters missed, antibiotics, fever-reducers, clean saline. Enough to save a family.

grantItem.name: case of medicine
grantItem.description: St. Brigid's committed medical stock, enough for the fever at Elkwater.

searchDetails.label: the ambulance doors
searchDetails.description: The old trail leads OUT the ambulance bay, but a second, fresher track doubles back INWARD and out again: someone in chalk-marked robes has been here since the rapture, and left carrying more than medicine. The Hollow Congregation walked these halls.

loc_warm_house.name: The Warm House
loc_warm_house.description: A family home taken mid-dinner, plates still set, chairs pushed back, a kettle boiled dry on a dead stove. No struggle, no blood; the scene is eerily whole. The trail out is the brightest and freshest your sight has found, still warm to look at. The neighbors down at Elkwater have one question and they will not stop asking it: which way did it go?

spawnOnEnter.name: The Thing That Was Born Here

loc_tithing_mill.name: The Tithing Mill
loc_tithing_mill.description: A moss-eaten timber mill that still runs, after a fashion, and a front. The mill-folk are the Hollow Congregation, and their tithe to the Lord below is betrayal: they guide neighbors onto 'safe' routes past the chaos, and chalk small marks on the doorposts of houses that, a week later, stand empty and pristine. The vanishings cluster too neatly along their recommended roads. There is no proof yet. But your sight reads a handler-scent on the chalk, and it matches the trails out of the taken homes.

meta.handlerScent: a cold oil-and-ash hand, the same that marked the doorposts of the taken houses

loc_drowned_highway.name: The Drowned Highway
loc_drowned_highway.description: An interstate slumped whole into a flooded valley, the roadbed gone under dark water and the roofs of drowned cars left as stepping stones across it. Something corrupted nests in the underpasses where the current pools. It is the fast way across the region and the stupid way to die, a shortcut you take when the long safe road costs more than you have.

loc_ranger_station_9.name: Ranger Station 9
loc_ranger_station_9.description: A fire-watch tower on a bald ridge, its walls still pinned with the intact regional maps the rangers kept, trailheads, drainages, the whole spread of the Verdance drawn by hands that knew it. A find worth the climb. But something roosts on the observation deck now, and it does not like the stairs used.

searchDetails.label: the pinned regional maps
searchDetails.description: Intact fire-watch maps pinned wall to wall, trailheads, drainages, the whole Verdance charted by hands that knew it.

grantItem.name: Ranger Station Maps
grantItem.description: The intact regional maps from Ranger Station 9, the Verdance charted node by node.

searchDetails.label: a ranger's pinned report
searchDetails.description: A field report pinned above the cot, hand underlined twice: something WALKS the Stillborn Field now. Not a chaosling. It wore a licensed ranger's face for a mile before the face came off. Rapture-born. Do not go alone. Do not go at all.

loc_reservoir.name: The Reservoir of the Unclaimed
loc_reservoir.description: Raptures consume energy but not memory, and the residue, everything the demons could not use, settles like silt, and water gathers it. The dam's glass-still reservoir is generations full of it. It remembers faces: at dusk the lost mouth their final moments across the flat black water. The keepers call the contents the Unclaimed. To your sight it is the region's only archive of what the vanished saw last, and grieving families will pay for a reading, though the Ledger frowns on selling the dead.

loc_champions_cairn.name: A Champion's Cairn
loc_champions_cairn.description: A burned ring in the moss, a rusted Legendary driven hilt-deep into the stone at its center, and bones buried beneath it, respectfully, by someone who came after. Whoever they were, She called them too, and it did not go well. Read the place and its vision plays like a prequel warning: a hero's last hour, offered to you as a lesson you would rather not need.

loc_unfinished_map.name: The Unfinished Map
loc_unfinished_map.description: A collapsed hide-camp, and in its ruin a hand-drawn map of the region, good work, real trails, real names, with margin notes that get shorter and stranger toward the end, about things 'she didn't warn me about.' The map is a gift. The notes are the beginning of a question you were not supposed to ask this early.

searchDetails.label: the hand-drawn map
searchDetails.description: A hand-drawn regional map, good work, real trails, real names, its charted heart of the Verdance exact even where the margin notes trail into dread.

grantItem.name: The Unfinished Map
grantItem.description: A hand-drawn chart of the Verdance, real trails, real names, honest as far as it goes.

loc_old_rapture.name: The Old Rapture
loc_old_rapture.description: A hamlet swallowed generations ago and gone all the way back to forest: doorframes grown into standing trees, a teacup cradled in a knot of roots, a chimney holding up a canopy. Nature has reclaimed the whole scene until it is almost beautiful. It is proof, if you needed it, that this war is ancient, and that the Verdance has done this grieving many times before.

loc_poachers_yard.name: The Poacher's Yard
loc_poachers_yard.description: An organized crew stripping the forest on an industrial schedule, flatbeds, chain hoists, a yard stacked with more than any honest need, and somehow dodging the Ledger, so far. The locals hate them and cannot touch them. But the light comes a half-beat wrong over the yard now, and the keepers say Her patience with a debt this large is visibly ending. A moral fork with a clock on it.

loc_bonelight_grove.name: The Bonelight Grove
loc_bonelight_grove.description: A luminous, chaos-touched stand where the trees glow the soft white of old bone and rare materials bloom in the understory, alchemy and craft reagents worth a fortune to anyone who can carry them out. So do the things that eat gatherers. The grove gives generously and collects without mercy.

loc_stillborn_field.name: The Stillborn Field
loc_stillborn_field.description: A failed rapture, the vanishing started and stopped halfway. Half-consumed echoes flicker at dusk, neither gone nor whole, caught mid-taking. The keepers forbid entry and cannot say why it happened; nobody understands it. Your sight, though, might read what stopped here, and that answer may matter more than anyone in the Verdance yet knows.

loc_choir_cave.name: The Choir Cave
loc_choir_cave.description: Karst galleries where chaos-saturated air, drawn through the stone throats of the cave, sings with stolen voices, playback of the consumed. A dead woman's verse. A counting-song in children's voices from the Old Rapture. Deeper still, the cave sings voices no one living has heard yet: it receives before the region loses. An early-warning oracle, or the cruelest lure ever set, the vanished singing your name from the dark. The richest essence outside the Cold Door, and the depth disorients everyone who reaches for it.

loc_cold_door.name: The Cold Door
loc_cold_door.description: A rapture that never cooled: a municipal cistern where the vanishing does not stop, it continues, slowly, still. Multiple strong guardian demons stand at it and never leave, exactly as the law of portals says they must. It is the region's known 'don't,' its single richest vein, and the place the Hollow Congregation delivers to. Everyone who matters agrees on one thing about the Cold Door: not yet.

loc_her_clearing.name: Her Clearing
loc_her_clearing.description: An unmarked meadow where the goddess manifests, the Elk's ground. You do not find it. It finds you, when She decides it should, and the finding is safe and terrifying in the same breath. Soft grass, level light, and the certainty, standing in it, that you are being read down to the seams.

opening.situation: You wake on the fringe of the Green Static at dawn, unarmed, in clothes that are not the ones you fell in. A town hums to the north; the ground around you is quiet and kept, and the shimmer above the far canopy marks the boundary of the wrong woods beyond. Then a voice that is not sound gives you a window and a choice.
authoredBeats:
The last of the old life is four seconds long: a restaurant, a glass set down, a bitter aftertaste that arrives too late, a floor tilting up to meet you. Then dark. Then wet pine needles against your cheek, cold coming up through the ground, and a body standing itself upright that is yours and isn't. Dawn light, filtered green and cold. You are unarmed, in clothes you have never owned. Somewhere north, a diesel generator coughs and catches.

You look around, the tree-line, the shimmer above the canopy, the flagpole past the trees, and then a VOICE arrives that does not use the air.

[ YOU ARE HEARD. ]

[ THIS WORLD IS CALLED WHAT YOURS WAS CALLED. IT IS OLDER THAN IT LOOKS. AT ITS SOUTH POLE STANDS A TOWER OF ONE HUNDRED FLOORS. IT HAS ALWAYS STOOD. IT IS CALLED BABEL, AND IT HAS BEEN LEFT UNATTENDED TOO LONG. ]

[ WHAT LEAKS FROM AN UNATTENDED TOWER IS CALLED CHAOS. IT SOAKS INTO GREEN THINGS AND RUNNING THINGS AND MAKES THEM WRONG. THE PEOPLE HERE FENCE IT, LICENSE IT, AND SELL TICKETS TO IT. THEY ARE MANAGING THE SYMPTOM. YOU ARE THE CURE. ]

[ YOUR BODY SLEEPS ELSEWHERE. IT IS NOT DEAD. DO NOT CALL IT DEAD, I DO NOT. YOUR SOUL CROSSED, AND I HAVE PLACED IT WHERE IT CAN GROW. YOUR FLESH HERE IS MORTAL, GUARD IT. WHAT YOU ARE IS NOT, BUT THERE ARE THINGS BELOW THE WORLD THAT EAT WHAT YOU ARE, AND YOU WILL MEET THEM LAST. ]
[ CLIMB. RECLAIM WHAT WAS LEFT OPEN. I WILL NOT SPEAK OFTEN, SO I GIVE YOU A WINDOW, THAT YOU NEED NOT TAKE MY WORD FOR ANYTHING. LOOK AT IT NOW. ]

[ THE WINDOW IS THE ONE THING I GIVE YOU TWICE. LEARN IT. IT HOLDS SIX MEASURES OF WHAT YOU ARE, STRENGTH, DEXTERITY, VITALITY, SPIRIT, INTELLECT, AND LUCK, AND BELOW THEM, YOUR SKILLS: THE THINGS YOU CAN DO. YOU HOLD NO SKILLS YET. THAT IS NOT A LACK. IT IS THE FIRST BLANK PAGE. ]

[ WHEN YOU DO NOT KNOW WHAT YOU ARE, OR WHERE YOU STAND, OR WHAT YOU HAVE BECOME, OPEN THE WINDOW AND READ. IT DOES NOT FLATTER. IT DOES NOT LIE. I WILL SPEAK RARELY. THE WINDOW IS ALWAYS OPEN, AND IT IS ENOUGH. ]

[ NORTH OF YOU: A TOWN OF THE LICENSED, WHERE THE PEOPLE WHO FENCE THE CHAOS KEEP THEIR PAPERS AND THEIR PRICES. AROUND YOU: THE WRONG WOODS, WHERE THE CHAOS IS NOT YET MANAGED. THE TOWN TEACHES WHAT THE LICENSED KNOW. THE WOODS TEACH WHAT YOU ARE. BOTH TEACH. CHOOSE. ]

The voice does not speak again. In the corner of your vision the STATUS window settles and holds, six measures, no skills yet, a rank the world has not read. North lies the road to Hollow Pine and its licensed doors. Around you lies the Static, un-fenced and waiting. Which way you go is the first thing you decide here, and the window will remember it.

dialogueBeats.label: Licensing
dialogueBeats.text: "You're new, and you're not on any roster I keep. Understand where that puts you: unlicensed, you're legally a bystander, you can't be paid for a cull, can't sell a core, can't file a zone claim. Testing's by appointment. The rig doesn't lie, and neither do I."

dialogueBeats.label: The zone
dialogueBeats.text: "EZ-44's a Class-D exclusion. Fringe is survivable if you respect it; the heart is where people stop coming back. We license the edge and pretend the middle is somebody else's problem."

dialogueBeats.label: The Stump
dialogueBeats.text: "One arm, one bar, every rumor in Hollow Pine, that's the going rate. You want work, the board's behind you. You want to know who's really running this town, buy a round and I'll tell you slower."

dialogueBeats.label: Salvage
dialogueBeats.text: "Cores, zone-tech, anything the Static spat out, I buy it. Sixty cents on the dollar, and before you scowl, that sixty is the only sixty anyone out here is offering. Charter's been paying double lately. Ask yourself why before you celebrate."

dialogueBeats.label: The clinic
dialogueBeats.text: "I patch hunters and I ask the questions that keep them alive. Some of those questions aren't mine, but the readings I take go where they do good, and you look like you've got a clean bill and a strange story. Sit. Let me look at you."

dialogueBeats.label: The neighbor
dialogueBeats.text: "Nine years I've lived out here, and the Static's the best neighbor I've had, it only takes what you bring it. You came in deep for someone with no license and no scars. That window behind your eyes, I've seen exactly one other, and it wasn't on a person. Sit down. I'll teach you what the edge won't."

cast.appearance: a hovering orb of warm green and gold light, a luminous ball of living radiance, soft golden-green glow, gentle rays, ethereal, no face, no body
cast.portraitPrompt: a single hovering ball of warm green and gold light, luminous glowing orb, soft radiant halo, ethereal, dark background, no face, no figure, no person

dialogueBeats.label: Rarely
dialogueBeats.text: [ I SAID I WOULD NOT SPEAK OFTEN. HEAR ME WHILE I DO. ]

offer_clearance.title: Clear the fringe
offer_clearance.summary: A posted bounty: cull a corrupted beast prowling the Static's fringe before it reaches the road.

quest_warm_house.title: The Warm House Hunt
quest_warm_house.summary: Something was born in the emptied Hale house and walked out. Follow the fresh trail before it circles back.

stages.objective: Follow the fresh trail from the Waking Mile to the Warm House.
The thing fled toward the Tithing Mill, corner what was born here, then follow where it leads.

quest_medicine_run.title: Elkwater's Notice, The Medicine Run
quest_medicine_run.summary: The Aldous children are dying of the ford-fever. St. Brigid's still has medicine on its shelves, if you'll risk the old rapture-site to bring it back.

stages.objective: Reach St. Brigid's Regional Medical and recover the committed medical stock.
Carry the medicine back to Elkwater Crossing before the fever takes the children.

factions.name: Root Shrine Keepers
factions.wants: The Tree honored, the Ledger taught, the Stillborn Field left sealed.

factions.name: The Hollow Congregation
factions.wants: The Lord's favor via tithed betrayals; feed and protect the Cold Door; remain unproven.

factions.name: Elkwater Crossing Community
factions.wants: Safety, medicine from St. Brigid's runs, news of the vanished, the traitor found.

factions.name: The Poacher's Yard Crew
factions.wants: Maximum extraction before consequences arrive; deniability.

fronts.title: The Salvage Clause
fronts.agenda: The Charter is moving to privatize exclusion-zone salvage, and EZ-44 is the pilot, the cores pay for the war, and the war is with the zone itself.

beats.label: Contrails and cash
beats.telegraph: Charter survey drones cross the Static's edge at dawn; by noon Vail's buy-prices have doubled with no explanation.
beats.brief: The Charter is surveying EZ-44 from the air and quietly bidding salvage prices up, someone means to own this zone's cores.
beats.decision: Cash in on the spike while it lasts, or find out what the Charter knows that you don't.

fact.text: Charter drones are mapping EZ-44 and salvage prices have doubled overnight, the zone's cores are about to become someone's property.

beats.label: The auction
beats.telegraph: A Charter notice goes up on the Stump's board: EZ-44 salvage rights, closed bid, independents need not apply.
beats.brief: The Charter announces closed-bid salvage rights to EZ-44, the independents who worked this zone are about to be fenced out of it.
beats.decision: Get licensed and bid useful to a Charter backer, or help the town's independents fight the clause.

fact.text: The Charter has put EZ-44's salvage rights to closed bid, Hollow Pine's independent salvagers are being priced out of their own zone.

fronts.title: The Warm House
fronts.agenda: A family was taken from the Warm House mid-dinner; the trail out is the freshest in the Verdance, and the neighbors at Elkwater want to know which way it went before it goes cold.

beats.label: The set table
beats.telegraph: At the Warm House the plates are still set and the kettle boiled dry, and to your sight the trail out the back door glows fresher than anything you have tracked.
beats.brief: A family was raptured from the Warm House within the day; the scene is whole and the essence-trail out is bright and recent, a bloodhound's dream and a mourner's nightmare.
beats.decision: Take the trail while it is warm, or leave the dead their privacy and walk away from the first fresh track you have found.

fact.text: The Warm House was raptured within the day, the scene left pristine, and the essence-trail out the back is the freshest the player has seen.

beats.label: Which way it went
beats.telegraph: The neighbors down at Elkwater press you with the only question that matters to them, and the trail you read bends toward the mill road.
beats.brief: Elkwater's watch wants to know which way the Warm House family went; the fresh trail bends toward the Tithing Mill's recommended 'safe' route.
beats.decision: Tell the neighbors what the trail says and where it leads, or keep what your sight read to yourself until you understand the mill road.

fact.text: The Warm House trail bends toward the Tithing Mill's recommended route, the same road the Hollow Congregation calls safe.

fronts.title: The Hollow Congregation
fronts.agenda: The mill-folk of the Tithing Mill are the Hollow Congregation, Tower-worshippers who buy the Lord's favor by betraying their own, guiding neighbors onto 'safe' routes that feed the Cold Door. They mean to stay unproven.

beats.label: The chalk marks
beats.telegraph: Fresh chalk marks appear on doorposts along the Mill's recommended 'safe' routes; a week later, one of those houses stands empty and pristine.
beats.brief: The Tithing Mill's 'safe' routes are marked in chalk, and the marked houses are the ones that vanish, the pattern is too neat to be chance, but there is no proof yet.
beats.decision: Warn the marked houses and tip your hand, or watch the marks and learn who is drawing them.

fact.text: Doorpost chalk marks along the Tithing Mill's recommended routes precede vanishings, the mill's 'safe' roads are a killing list.

beats.label: The handler-scent
beats.telegraph: Your sight snags on the chalk: the same handler-scent that clings to the fresh trails out of the taken homes is on the marks themselves.
beats.brief: The essence handler-scent on the doorpost chalk matches the trails out of raptured homes, the Congregation is not hiding from the demons; it is working with what leaves them, and the Mill supplies the Cold Door.
beats.decision: Take the read to Elkwater's watch as the traitor they have been hunting, or follow the mill road to the Cold Door and see the tithe delivered.

fact.text: The handler-scent on the Congregation's chalk matches the rapture trails, the Tithing Mill betrays its neighbors to the Cold Door for the Lord's favor.

secrets.text: The Stag's core is worth a season of Vail's patience, and the Charter's doubled prices are a bet that someone will bring it in before the clause locks the zone.
The Warm House trail doesn't wander like a drifting demon's. It goes straight for the mill road, because someone walked the family there on purpose.
The Congregation isn't hiding from the raptures. It's feeding them, neighbors tithed to the Cold Door for a favor from the thing at the bottom of the Tower.

placements.note: The Limping Grey, authored starter encounter, placed on the first exit from the Green Static fringe.
Bandit ambush: enforcers hold a dry span of the Drowned Highway underpass, running a toll. Human threats, no essence trail.
The Knife leads the Drowned Highway toll-crew; a parley window opens at their morale break.
VERY RARE. A drifted rapture-born walks the Stillborn Field fringe. Discoverable via rumor; never forced.
